title: Promptless CLI
description: A command-line companion that surfaces Promptless suggestions, lints LLM prose tells, and exposes agent-readable views of your published pages from the terminal.

title: Promptless Overview
description: Learn what Promptless does, how it works, and where to start.

title: Command reference
description: Every command the Promptless CLI exposes — authentication, suggestions, local linting, web tools, shell completions, and the conventions shared across them.

title: Introducing Promptless 1.0: Solving the hardest problems in docs automation
description: Promptless automatically updates your docs as you ship features and support customers, keeping your docs current without the constant overhead.

title: Suggestions
description: Use promptless suggestions to list, filter, and inspect the docs updates Promptless has drafted for your organization, with `git diff --stat` summaries and JSON output for scripting.

title: Configuration
description: How the Promptless CLI stores its API key, resolves the config file path, and applies file permissions.

title: Environment variables
description: Every environment variable the Promptless CLI reads, with defaults, formats, and usage examples.

title: CLI auth protocol
description: The contract between the Promptless CLI, web app, and backend for the login flow, including the v1 RSA-OAEP key delivery format.

title: Developer quickstart
description: How to verify the Promptless CLI end-to-end against the bundled fake-server fixture, including the four agent-runnable test specs for the auth surface.

title: Customizing Notifications
description: Configure how Promptless notifies your team about documentation suggestions

title: Doc Detective Integration
description: Test documentation workflows against your actual product with Promptless and Doc Detective

title: How to use Promptless
description: Learn about the different ways to interact with Promptless and leverage its documentation automation capabilities

title: Working with Slack
description: Learn how to use Promptless directly in Slack through message actions, DMs, channel mentions, and passive listening

title: Interacting with Promptless PRs
description: Learn how to provide follow-up instructions through GitHub PR comments and reviews after your documentation PR is opened

title: Using the Web Interface
description: Learn how to use Promptless's web interface to highlight text, provide feedback, and guide documentation improvements

title: Providing Feedback
description: Learn all the ways to provide feedback on Promptless documentation suggestions

title: Deep Analysis
description: Submit large, complex documentation requests that require extended research and may create multiple suggestions across your docs

title: Agent Knowledge Base
description: View and edit the files that help Promptless understand your documentation style, product context, and workflow preferences

title: Self-Hosting Overview
description: Deploy Promptless on your own infrastructure for enhanced security, compliance, and control

title: Kubernetes + Helm Deployment
description: Complete guide for deploying Promptless on Kubernetes using Helm charts

title: Technical Writing with AI: Faster Drafts, Larger Maintenance Surface
description: AI tools have made technical writing faster. They've also created more documentation to maintain, with the same capacity to keep it accurate.

title: Your Docs Are Pages. Your Product Knowledge Is a Graph.
description: Flat docs lose the relationships between your product's concepts. Here's why a living knowledge graph matters for teams building with AI agents.

title: Documentation Versioning Best Practices for API Teams
description: Versioned docs multiply your maintenance work with every release. Here's how API teams can plan for it before it becomes a support problem.

title: Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your Engineering Team Has Never Checked
description: Your engineering team tracks test coverage. Almost no one tracks documentation coverage. Here's how to measure whether your product surface is actually documented.

title: Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your AI Features Actually Need
description: Most docs teams track page count and traffic. Here's why documentation coverage is the metric that actually predicts whether your AI features will work.

title: Interactive API Documentation: Why the Hard Part Is Keeping It Accurate
description: Most teams ship interactive API docs and stop there. Here's why spec drift undermines the "try it out" experience, and what changes when AI agents are involved.

title: Ship Your First (or Next) Open Source Docs PR
description: Come work through pre-vetted documentation issues from real open-source projects at Write the Docs Portland 2026 Writing Day. Projects include Mautic, Vitess, and Helm.

title: How to Connect Code Repositories with Documentation Platforms
description: If you're looking to connect a docs repo to Fern, Readme, GitBook, or Mintlify — that's easy and each platform covers it. This is about the harder problem: connecting your product code so docs stay current when the product changes.

title: Test spec: config file paths
description: How the Promptless CLI resolves the config file location across the developer override, XDG, and default paths.

title: Test spec: env-var precedence
description: How PROMPTLESS_CLI_API_SECRET in the environment overrides the cached config file across login, whoami, and bogus-token scenarios.

title: Test spec: logout edge cases
description: Logout behaviors — preserving unrelated keys, deleting empty files, idempotency, and env-var warnings.

title: Test spec: authentication happy path
description: Step-by-step spec for the promptless login → whoami → logout flow against a local fake-server fixture.

description: If you're looking to connect a docs repo to Fern, Readme, GitBook, or Mintlify — that's easy and each platform covers it. This is about the harder problem: connecting your product code so docs stay current when the product changes.

title: SDK Documentation Best Practices: What Actually Hurts Developer Adoption
description: SDK documentation fails developers in predictable ways. Here's how to cut Time to First Call and keep code examples accurate as your SDK evolves.

title: Connect Multiple GitHub Organizations to One Promptless Account
description: Teams with multiple GitHub organizations can now connect them all to a single Promptless account, manage each integration independently, and see repos from every org in one project dropdown.

title: Edit Doc Collection Settings Without Contacting Support
description: Doc collection settings can now be edited directly in the Promptless dashboard. Update your docs framework, config path, published URL, Vale config, or Doc Detective settings without a support request.

title: Edit Doc Collection Settings Without Contacting Support
description: Doc collection settings can now be edited directly in the Promptless dashboard. Update your docs framework, config path, published URL, Vale config, or Doc Detective settings without a support request.

title: SDK Documentation Best Practices That Hold Up After Launch
description: SDK documentation best practices mean maintaining three distinct surfaces that break differently: reference, code samples, and getting-started guides.

title: API Changelog Best Practices: Write for the Developer, Not the Team
description: 68% of developers cite outdated API docs as their top frustration. Most changelogs cause it. Here's how to write one developers can actually use.

title: What Documentation Debt Actually Costs Engineering Teams
description: Documentation debt generates no automated alerts. Here's how to measure what you can't see and stop paying the hidden cost in onboarding and engineering time.

title: Documentation Debt Accrues Where Your Team Can't See It
description: Documentation debt quietly costs mid-sized engineering teams $500K–$2M annually. The people bearing that cost aren't in your standups. Here's how to prioritize what to fix.

title: How to Measure Developer Documentation ROI
description: Most documentation teams track outputs, not outcomes. Here's how to measure developer documentation ROI, and why that return decays if docs aren't maintained.

title: Comment @promptless on a PR to Request Documentation
description: Promptless can now be triggered by commenting @promptless on a pull request in any repo you've connected to Promptless, including merged or closed ones.

title: Developer Documentation ROI: The Metrics That Actually Matter
description: Most teams make the documentation ROI case with support tickets. That understates it by 10x. Here's how to measure what leadership actually cares about.

title: Promptless Now Supports Starlight (Astro) as a Docs Platform
description: Promptless now supports Starlight, the Astro-based docs framework, with automatic detection via astro.config files and first-class onboarding support.

title: We Scored 100 on Agent-Friendly Docs. Here's Why That's Not Enough.
description: Making your docs accessible to AI agents is necessary but not sufficient. Agents are more credulous than humans, which means accuracy and clarity matter more than ever.

title: We Scored 100 on Agent-Friendly Docs. Here's Why That's Not Enough.
description: Making your docs accessible to AI agents is necessary but not sufficient. Agents are more credulous than humans, which means accuracy and clarity matter more than ever.

title: Using Slack as a "Knowledge Base"? Here's What You're Missing out on
description: Slack feels like a knowledge store because answers happen there. Its design actively destroys institutional knowledge. Here's why docs-as-code fixes it.

title: Why Developer Onboarding Documentation Breaks After Launch
description: 44% of developers dig through source code because onboarding docs don't match the product. Here's why accurate docs drift into inaccuracy — and how to catch it.

title: Karpathy's LLM Wiki Went Viral. Here's What It Means for Your Personal Knowledge Base.
description: Karpathy's LLM wiki pattern solves the oldest problem in personal knowledge management. Here's why it matters for your personal wiki, idea file, or knowledge base.

title: When YOU are the Docs Team - Lessons from Working with Some of the Best Writers and Managers in the Industry
description: Whether you're the only technical writer at your company or leading the docs team, some of the structural challenges are similar: not enough resources to build infrastructure, a manager who doesn't know your craft, and an AI moment that's changing what the job entails.

title: Preview Rendered Markdown Diffs in the Dashboard
description: Promptless now shows a rendered preview of Markdown and MDX suggestion diffs. Click "Preview Markdown" on any .md or .mdx file to see changes as formatted output, not raw diff text.

title: Preview Rendered Markdown Diffs in the Dashboard
description: Promptless now shows a rendered preview of Markdown and MDX suggestion diffs. Click "Preview Markdown" on any .md or .mdx file to see changes as formatted output, not raw diff text.

title: Help Center vs Docs-as-Code: When to Switch
description: A practical guide to choosing between a help center and docs-as-code, with specific signals for when to migrate and when to stay.

title: Which Agent Memory Provider Should You Choose, and Why Memory Alone is not Enough
description: A practical guide to choosing an agent memory provider by workload, with clear tradeoffs, technical specs, and what to add so reliability holds up in production.

title: llms.txt Is Not What Most Teams Think It Is
description: llms.txt won't boost your AI search rankings. But for developer-facing companies, there's a different—and real—reason to care about it.

title: How to Increase the Deflection Rate of Your Support Agent and Build a Feedback Loop
description: Most support agent deflection problems aren't model problems — they're documentation problems. Here's how to diagnose yours and build a feedback loop that compounds over time.

title: What is Agent Context Engineering? And How is it Different from Prompt and Harness Engineering?
description: Learn what agent context engineering is, how it differs from prompt and harness engineering, why most agent failures are context failures, and a four-layer framework for building reliable AI agents.

title: Documentation Drift Is a Detection Problem, Not a Writing Problem
description: Most teams treat documentation drift as a writing problem. It's not. The real bottleneck is detection — knowing what changed before the damage is done.

title: Documentation Metrics and Analytics: A Complete Guide for Developer-Facing Companies
description: Learn which documentation metrics actually matter for developer-facing companies, how to measure them, and how to connect doc quality to business outcomes.

title: What It Looks Like When Your Docs Keep Up With Your Product
description: Three real workflows from teams using Promptless that show what changes when your tooling handles detection, context gathering, and the first draft.

title: A Practical Guide on How to Optimize Your Docs for Agents
description: A practical guide for technical writers on adapting documentation for AI agent consumption, grounded in how agents actually access and use docs today.

title: Ambient Capture: The Missing Layer in Modern Documentation
description: Ambient capture is the intelligent listening and recommendation layer for docs owners, continuously detecting docs-impacting changes so writers can focus on analysis and communication rather than surveillance.

title: Why we're building Promptless
description: AI won't replace human labor. But people that use AI well will replace those that don't. Promptless exists to make sure as many people as possible are in the first group.

title: Parrots are underrated pets and tools
description: You know dozens of useful heuristics but can't hold them all in your head while doing actual work. LLMs can run those obvious-in-hindsight checks in parallel in the background, acting as cognitive prosthetics that apply known wisdom continuously so you don't have to remember to.

title: Writing code was hard, actually
description: The revisionist claim that writing code was never the hard part arrives conveniently at the moment AI makes it cheap to produce. But the market, the machine, and the engineers who built it all tell a different story.

title: "I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream"
description: What happens when you give an AI agent a Slack channel to complain in.

title: Launch Week: December 2025
description: Five major features we launched this December, from custom voice matching to automated screenshot updates.

title: Running a Lightweight Research Paper Club
description: How we run a weekly paper club at Promptless that's surprisingly useful, surprisingly easy, and has led to tangible product improvements.

title: How Vellum uses Promptless to draft over 50% of their doc updates
description: By democratizing documentation contributions, every team member now drives content—making docs a true team sport powered by Promptless.
